name: Habib ibn Mazahir
title: Elder of the Companions
role: Commander of the Army's Left Wing
shortDescription: A 75-year-old companion of the Prophet and close friend of Imam Ali who secretly came to Karbala at night to assist the Imam.
fullStory:
Habib ibn Mazahir al-Asadi was one of the trusted elders and prominent figures of the city of Kufa. Having lived during the youth of the Prophet of Islam (PBUH), he was considered one of his companions and subsequently one of the very close and special companions of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). His faith, piety, and brilliant track record among the people of Kufa earned him special respect from everyone.

After Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad was appointed as the governor of Kufa and blocked the city's exit routes by creating severe fear and terror to prevent anyone from going to aid Imam Hussain (AS), conditions became very difficult for the lovers of the Ahl al-Bayt. Nevertheless, having received a letter from Imam Hussain (AS), Habib decided to reach the Imam of his time at any cost. He left the city completely secretly at night, bypassing the main roads and escaping Ibn Ziyad's patrols to reach the desert of Karbala.

The arrival of Habib ibn Mazahir in Karbala created a wave of joy and encouragement among the small caravan of Imam Hussain (AS), and especially among the women of the camp. Upon seeing the massive enemy army, he asked the Imam for permission to go to his tribe (Bani Asad), who were near Karbala, and invite them to help the Imam. Although a group of them accepted, they were dispersed by an attack from Umar Sa'd's forces, and Habib returned to the Imam alone.

The documented and precise report of the book Al-Irshad written by Sheikh Mufid states that on the morning of Ashura, when the Kufan army formed their battle lines, Imam Hussain (AS) also arranged his very small army for defense. With complete trust in the capability and loyalty of his companions, he appointed Habib ibn Mazahir as the commander of the left wing of the army and Zuhayr ibn Qayn over the right wing, handing the main standard to his brother Abbas.

Sheikh Mufid continues his report in Al-Irshad, writing: When Husayn ibn Tamim (one of the enemy commanders) taunted the Imam's companions, Habib answered him with utmost bravery and firmness before heading to the battlefield. Despite his old age (around 75 years), he fought heroically and struck down many enemies. Finally, a man from the Banu Tamim tribe named Budayl ibn Suraym dealt a heavy blow to his head with a sword, and another man from the Tamim tribe knocked him to the ground with a spear. The martyrdom of Habib deeply saddened Imam Hussain (AS), and the Imam, with immense sorrow, stood by his side and said: 'I seek my reward and the reward of my supportive companions from God'.
quoteArabic: وَ اللهِ لَو عَلِمتُ أَنِّي أُقتَلُ، ثُمَّ أُحيَى، ثُمَّ أُحرَقُ حَيّاً، ثُمَّ أُذَرَّى، يُفعَلُ ذلِكَ بِي سَبعِينَ مَرَّةً، مَا فَارَقتُكَ يَا حُسَين
quote: By God, if I knew I would be killed, then revived, and this was done to me seventy times, I would never separate from you, O Hussain.
